pages.raw_text:
Real estate men in Spokane re-
port a general picking up in their
line of business during the last
week. Much attention is being paid
to outlying residence property and
to fruit land adjacent to the city.
Within the city limits most of the
sales are confined to the small resi-
dence properties, and many home
seekers are still coming into the
city, and the small North Side resi-
dence property is declared to be
most in demand at the present time.


Dickson & Dunn, of the Jameson
building, report the sale Of 10 acres
of the Dickson-Dunn orchard tracts
just this side Of Mead, to Barney
Dunn of Colebrook, N. H., during
the last week. and of another acre
tract to DR.. c. e. Burkett of this
city. The sales amounted to about
$4,000. Mr.. Dunn of New Hamp-
shire will build a six room house
on his place.


A small section of the Dickson
Dunn orchard tracts bordering on
Mead has been cut up into city
lots, which are being sold as Dick
son-Dunn's First addition to Mead.
